Need be updated to reportBadDefinition or another exception handler.

title: Jackson 2.15.0 - Error deserializing an @JsonUnwrapped object with a field of type float
author: amombourquette
body:
This works as of Jackson 2.14.2, and fails with Jackson 2.15.0, both using bson4jackson 2.13.1

Example class being deserialized: (groovy syntax)
```
static class OuterClass {
    @JsonUnwrapped
    public InnerClass inner = new InnerClass()
    OuterClass() {}
}

static class InnerClass {
    public float floatValue = 40.0f
    public InnerClass() {}
}
```

Failing test (but passes with older jackson):
```
OuterClass outer = new OuterClass()
outer.inner.floatValue = 50.0f
ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
ObjectMapper BSON_MAPPER = new ObjectMapper(new BsonFactory())
BSON_MAPPER.writeValue(baos, outer)
ByteArrayInputStream bais = new ByteArrayInputStream(baos.toByteArray())
OuterClass clone = BSON_MAPPER.readValue(bais, OuterClass.class)
assertThat(clone.inner.floatValue).isEqualTo(outer.inner.floatValue)
```

I am uncertain if the actual bug exists in jackson or bson4jackson, but the following test using only jackson passes for both old and new versions: (This will use a ReaderBasedJsonParser instead of a BsonParser)
```
ObjectMapper MAPPER = new ObjectMapper()
OuterClass outer = MAPPER.readValue("{\"floatValue\":50.0}", OuterClass.class)
assertThat(outer.inner.floatValue).isEqualTo(50.0f)
```

One relevant change in jackson is in `TokenBuffer._copyBufferValue(JsonParser p, JsonToken t)`
[2.14 version](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/blob/d47d1b642931246770ec67c4a98ad6aacc0f7d7d/src/main/java/com/fasterxml/jackson/databind/util/TokenBuffer.java#L1243) vs [2.15 version](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/blob/df541d3c74133496454dd9bfccc404b54a0faea6/src/main/java/com/fasterxml/jackson/databind/util/TokenBuffer.java#L1274)
2.14 version calls JsonParser.getNumberValueExact() which was overridden in BsonParser, which gets the value out of BsonParser.Context.value 
2.15 version calls JsonParser.getNumberValueDeferred() which is not implemented by BsonParser, so it calls ParserBase.
getNumberValueDeferred(), which gets the value from _numberString, _numberFloat, _textBuffer as needed.

title: UTF-8 conversion is significantly slower with BSON than regular JSON
author: MartinHaeusler
body:
Hi there,

I just did some quick performance testing in order to test the performance of BSON against JSON (and kryo). I noticed something that may be relevant for you. In my (simplistic) test, BSON took 3 times as long to serialize the same workload as JSON (both with `writeValueAsBytes`). Upon closer inspection, it seems that the UTF-8 conversion of the field names is at fault.

Here's what JVisualVM has to say about JSON:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16356998/165171311-b266d5ca-fc79-4916-a792-8b380ea8ff9d.png)

... and (same workload) for BSON:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16356998/165171358-641e44fc-abac-4a83-bac4-4675f7fe6bbe.png)

Expanding the details on the BSON variant furhter always ends up at the same method: `DynamicOutputBuffer.putUTF8(...)` calling into `java.nio.charset.CharsetEncoder.encode(...)`. That's where the time is lost in comparison to the blank JSON variant.

This is quite a significant difference and I thought I should at least report it.

Here's the full project which produced the above results (it's in Kotlin; just run the static `main` method of `Main.kt`)
[BsonTest.zip](https://github.com/michel-kraemer/bson4jackson/files/8557858/BsonTest.zip)

I might be wrong with my analysis, I was merely experimenting, so please take it with a grain of salt.

title: Decimal128 should extend Number & implement Comparable
author: kajsa
body: Per the comment in `de.undercouch.bson4jackson.types.Decimal128`, this class was copied from the mongo java driver. `org.mongodb:bson` has a more usable version that extends `Number` and implements `Comparable<Decimal128>`.

title: ObjectId no longer compatible with mongodb ObjectId
author: Dayde
body:
As of mongodb driver 4.x the previously deprecated method ObjectId.createFromLegacyFormat has been removed.

MongoDB ObjectId constructor with timestamp, machine and counter no longer exists.

Thus it is now incompatible with the ObjectId as serialized in bson4jackson library.

I have been able to write a workaround by reimplementing the former mongodb way of creating the hex string of an objectid https://github.com/Maltcommunity/jongo/commit/5e792c142e40ca90bee390f6bcb449fc5b5a1f95

But it would be great not to have to do that :)

title: Support for Jackson 2.12
author: hisener
body:
Hey, is there any plan to support Jackson 2.12?

We found one incompatibility with bson4jackson 2.11 and Jackson 2.12. In https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/issues/631, they introduced `JsonParser#getNumberValueExact` method. I think `BsonParser` should override that method and delegate to `BsonParser#getNumberValue`.

https://github.com/michel-kraemer/bson4jackson/blob/344ea73d509710f8c0082dbec669a6903dc1b412/src/main/java/de/undercouch/bson4jackson/BsonParser.java#L703-L706

title: com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.MismatchedInputException: No content to map due to end-of-input
author: HongZhaoHua
body:
Is bson4jackson supported Primitive/Wrapper type as root element now?

I write a test case like this:

    @Test
    public void test() throws Exception {
        ObjectMapper typeConverter = new ObjectMapper(new BsonFactory());
        byte[] value = typeConverter.writeValueAsBytes(true);
        Assert.assertTrue(typeConverter.readValue(value, Boolean.class));
    }

but it will throws a Exception.
